dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4793 IS: Foreign-Trade Zone Export Enhancement Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Scott of South Carolina (for himself and Mrs. Britt) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To clarify provisions of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement Implementation Act and the Foreign Trade Zones Act with respect to the appropriate tariff treatment of merchandise in a United States foreign-trade zone, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Foreign-Trade Zone Export Enhancement Act of 2026.
The purpose of this Act is to ensure that foreign-trade zones in the United States continue—

paragraph.text: to operate in support of United States competitiveness in manufacturing and distribution; and
to create and preserve jobs in the United States.

section.header: Rules related to duties imposed on foreign-trade zone merchandise

subsection.header: Amendments to Foreign Trade Zones Act
subsection.text: Section 3 of the Act of June 18, 1934 (commonly known as the Foreign Trade Zones Act) (19 U.S.C. 81c), is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Export from a foreign-Trade zone of certain merchandise manufactured or changed in condition
subsection.text: Notwithstanding the seventh proviso of subsection (a)—

paragraph.text: each article subject to the procedures described in such proviso and classifiable under the heading 9801.00.95 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States shall enter free of duty in accordance with such heading; and
any article consisting of components subject to such procedures and classifiable under such heading shall enter free of duty with respect to such components.

subsection.text: Subchapter I of chapter 98 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States is amended by inserting in numerical sequence the following new subheading:

row.entry: Merchandise that is subject to duty deferral restrictions of the free trade agreement between the United States, Mexico, and Canada (19 U.S.C. 4501, et seq.; commonly known as the USMCA) (or any successor agreement) that is admitted into a United States foreign-trade zone, manufactured, or changed in condition therein, and subsequently withdrawn for direct exportation to the territory of a Party to that agreement

section.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection shall issue such regulations as are necessary to implement section 3.
